enablement.status: read-only foundation; conquest play not yet enabled

territories.name: Baltimore-Washington Corridor
territories.uncertainty: Washington-Alexandria water access does not prove large-army lift or erase the rail break.

nonLinks.claim: Potomac access does not replace the Washington-Alexandria rail transfer or prove through transport.

territories.name: Upper Potomac-Harpers Ferry
territories.uncertainty: Repeated control change is evidence, not a predetermined player-state transition.

nonLinks.claim: Canal, river, road, and rail co-location does not create an automatic multimode service.

territories.name: Northern Virginia-Manassas
territories.uncertainty: Line use and destruction varied by campaign; the territory row carries no service state.

nonLinks.claim: Alexandria and Manassas membership does not imply an always-open corridor.

territories.name: Shenandoah Valley-Strasburg
territories.uncertainty: Strasburg is the evidenced terminus for this registry.

nonLinks.claim: Strasburg-Harpers Ferry remains an explicit rail non-link.

territories.name: Richmond Capital District
territories.uncertainty: Richmond water reach is bounded by passage evidence and cannot be inferred from the city endpoint alone.

nonLinks.claim: Drewry's Bluff is outside the Richmond endpoint and belongs to CT-08's bounded approach.

territories.name: Petersburg-City Point
territories.uncertainty: Landing, railhead, river staging reach, and city control require separate evidence and later state.

nonLinks.claim: City Point landing control does not automatically confer Petersburg control or through movement.

territories.name: Virginia Southside-Lynchburg
territories.uncertainty: Late-war supply and retreat evidence does not establish uninterrupted through service.

nonLinks.claim: Shared territory membership does not collapse the distinct Lynchburg and Danville rail corridors.

territories.name: Hampton Roads-Peninsula
territories.uncertainty: Arrival, York/James reach, withdrawal, and passage control are distinct.

nonLinks.claim: Fort Monroe arrival does not create a Yorktown transport edge.

territories.name: North Carolina Sounds-Weldon
territories.uncertainty: New Bern is one-family reach evidence; draft, season, and full sounds connectivity remain unresolved.

nonLinks.claim: The packet authorizes no complete North Carolina sounds graph.

territories.name: Cape Fear-Wilmington
territories.uncertainty: Offshore arrival, fort capture, runner closure, harbor use, city capture, and rail control have separate states.

nonLinks.claim: Fort Fisher approach closure does not automatically confer Wilmington city or rail control.

territories.name: Charleston-Port Royal Lowcountry
territories.uncertainty: Port Royal base and survey evidence is one family and does not prove routine neighboring-port reach.

nonLinks.claim: Port Royal does not create a Charleston or Savannah army-transfer edge.

territories.name: Savannah-Coastal Georgia
territories.uncertainty: Savannah-Beaufort transfer rests on one family and explicitly describes bounded lift.

nonLinks.claim: The one-family Beaufort transfer is not routine, instantaneous, or automatically bidirectional.

territories.name: Augusta-Upper Savannah
territories.uncertainty: Station and gauge continuity toward Charleston and the Carolinas requires later verification.

nonLinks.claim: No seamless Charleston-Carolinas interchange is implied.

territories.name: Atlanta-North Georgia
territories.uncertainty: Four documented railroad families meet here, but this territory row creates no interchange or service.

nonLinks.claim: A hub label does not combine separate rail rows or their changing wartime condition.

territories.name: Macon-Central Georgia
territories.uncertainty: Late-war rerouting is documented qualitatively; exact through service is unresolved.

nonLinks.claim: Rerouting evidence does not create a universal Macon through-service.

territories.name: Montgomery-Central Alabama
territories.uncertainty: West Point transfer and gauge continuity remain uncertain.

nonLinks.claim: West Point and Montgomery membership does not prove seamless through service.

territories.name: Mobile Bay-Lower Alabama
territories.uncertainty: Entrance passage, blockade-runner closure, harbor use, city control, and rail operation have distinct dates.

nonLinks.claim: Mobile Bay entrance control does not automatically confer Mobile city or rail control.

territories.name: Louisville-Ohio Falls
territories.uncertainty: Co-located rail and Ohio River evidence does not establish same-order transfer or capacity.

nonLinks.claim: Louisville rail-water interchange requires its own claim; co-location alone is insufficient.

territories.name: Cairo-Paducah-Western Kentucky
territories.uncertainty: Columbus and Island No. 10 include one-family evidence and special passage constraints.

nonLinks.claim: Island No. 10's gunboat and transport passage paths may not be collapsed into one routine edge.

territories.name: Nashville-Middle Tennessee
territories.uncertainty: Fort Donelson passage, Nashville control, port handling, and rail operation remain separate facts.

nonLinks.claim: Nashville rail-water co-location does not prove same-order transfer or intact service.

territories.name: Chattanooga-East Tennessee
territories.uncertainty: The Tennessee River port is documented, while Muscle Shoals bounds reach.

nonLinks.claim: Muscle Shoals is a bounded reach, not a through water edge.

territories.name: Tennessee River-Shiloh
territories.uncertainty: Fort Henry opening and Pittsburg Landing use are dated events, not a permanent service declaration.

nonLinks.claim: A landing and an opened reach do not establish universal capacity or year-round passage.

territories.name: Memphis-West Tennessee
territories.uncertainty: The water node is one-family; city co-location does not prove a functioning interchange.

nonLinks.claim: Memphis rail-water interchange is not established by shared place text.

territories.name: Corinth-North Mississippi
territories.uncertainty: The documented junction and control interval do not create permanent operation.

nonLinks.claim: Junction status does not merge the Memphis-Charleston and Mobile-Ohio services.

territories.name: Vicksburg-Mississippi Bend
territories.uncertainty: Vessel passage, downstream rendezvous, landing, siege, surrender, and through navigation are separate.

nonLinks.claim: Battery passage and Bruinsburg landing do not confer Vicksburg control or routine through service.

territories.name: Jackson-Central Mississippi
territories.uncertainty: Exact repair and reopening intervals remain unresolved.

nonLinks.claim: A mapped line does not prove continuous wartime operation or a repair duration.

territories.name: Meridian-East Mississippi
territories.uncertainty: The territory row identifies the node only; it carries no opening, repair, or bypass state.

nonLinks.claim: Map presence alone does not prove uninterrupted Meridian through-service.

territories.name: Lower Mississippi-New Orleans
territories.uncertainty: Fort passage, port surrender, occupation, Port Hudson, and through navigation retain separate dates.

nonLinks.claim: New Orleans occupation does not immediately open the Mississippi through Vicksburg and Port Hudson.

territories.name: Missouri River-St. Louis
territories.uncertainty: Territory presence comes from the existing Missouri and border-war library, not the conquest transport packets.

nonLinks.claim: D497 and D499 authorize no rail or water service for this territory.

territories.name: Missouri Ozarks
territories.uncertainty: The Wilson's Creek-Pea Ridge research family supports territory presence but no conquest-packet service.

nonLinks.claim: No packet-unsupported rail or water service is implied.

territories.name: Lower Arkansas-Arkansas Post
territories.uncertainty: Lower White-Arkansas evidence is one family and supports only the bounded Arkansas Post reach.

nonLinks.claim: No upper White River or Little Rock through edge is authorized.

territories.name: Little Rock-Central Arkansas
territories.uncertainty: The cited approach failed in low water; neither rail-water interchange nor through passage is established.

nonLinks.claim: Little Rock remains an explicit bounded water non-link; a rail node at the city does not manufacture a water edge.

territories.name: Alexandria-Lower Red River
territories.uncertainty: The 1864 low-water emergency proves a corridor-specific limit, not a reusable season table.

nonLinks.claim: Low-water failure and Bailey-dam withdrawal do not become routine seasonal or repair rules.

territories.name: Shreveport-North Louisiana
territories.uncertainty: Shreveport was an objective; the combined force did not establish routine large-force water reach.

nonLinks.claim: No accomplished large-force upper Red River edge is authorized.

territories.name: Marshall-East Texas
territories.uncertainty: The Louisiana gap remains physical, and the alleged Neches-area rail removal is disputed.

nonLinks.claim: The Louisiana gap is not bridged; the disputed removal claim cannot establish or erase service.

territories.name: Houston-Galveston
territories.uncertainty: Rail presence is supported; the strategic-water packet deliberately omits Texas-coast passage.

nonLinks.claim: No Texas-coast strategic-water edge is authorized.
